text:
Market today: The tech and chip sector shows mixed signals. Super Micro's unexpected margin surge and new orders are very positive for chip demand, but the Fed's alarm on an AI model hints at future regulatory scrutiny. Jamie Dimon's bearish bond call adds a layer of macro caution.

Your holdings: SOXL is down a painful -37.71% and SPAL a staggering -48.51%; both are leveraged ETFs, amplifying these drops on margin. These require immediate attention. QQQI is also flagged to TRIM and is close to its stop at 52.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the broader market's reaction to Dimon's bond comments and any further news on AI regulation. Super Micro's momentum might spill over to other chip names.

Bottom line: Prioritize aggressive risk management on your leveraged, underwater positions.
plays: Your -37.71% loss on leveraged SOXL is critical; consider trimming or selling to reduce exposure and protect capital.
The -48.51% loss on leveraged SPAL is alarming for a new position; re-evaluate its role and consider cutting losses.
With Super Micro's strong chip news, consider NVDA long. Scanner entry: 206.47-206.24, stop: 205.57.
